reviews.review: Mine works and looks good, but I can find anyone to shoot at.

I'm a SH3 and SH4 veteran. This game is nothing like them. It disappoints in a lot of ways, most importantly the weird interaction thing, and an enormous collection of bugs. Bugs include somehow getting stuck at the bridge, being unable to change positions, crewmen drowning themselves, etc. Even modding the game is a huge hassle, and still leaves a lot of bugs to be fixed. It is simply not worth the trouble.
If you wish to get started on submarine simulation, just go for SH3 with Grey Wolves or SH4 with TriggerMaru. For the Atlantic side of the war, SH3 beats this game hands down in every aspect(except perhaps graphics, and even there, SH5 is no stunner), and is cheaper to boot.
Its pretty fun worth it if its on sale
Old but still best sub game in market, no stability issues.

I know many people will say that this game is not the best and they are right, if you want the better silent hunters then go back to 3 and 4 but I do like this more modern game for its new features and gameplay. while the computer does do most of the work for you, its still fun to have the feeling of a submarine captain and causing death and chaos on the open sea's. this game isn't bad, its just different from the old one's and I enjoy for what it is.

This game can be a lot of fun if you mod it. The simple way to do that is to get The Wolves of Steel mega mod which now contains a patcher that works with the Steam version. The mod will allow you to play the game as hardcore sim as you want but also allows easy targeting that you don't see in some of the videos on line about the game. For those who want the game in spoken English be sure to back up your steam game before modding it you will need the voice files because TWOS mod overwrites the English with German.  As for all the comments about the game being broken, well yes out of the box you will never get past the first campaign, with modding you get the whole war from 1939 on. With some of the radio and music mods that you can add you can hear BBC broadcasts that happened during the war in the years that they happened. It can be a very immersive fun game but it can be a challenge to mod  which is why TWOS mod was created.
I don't know why this game won't launch

Realized I never wrote a review for this game. I played this game when it first came out.. It was a steaming pile of buggy mess.. I then forgot about it and continued playing SH4. Fast forward nearly a decade. I decide to come back. Only to find.. It's still just as crappy... 

First thing most notable is the complete lack of any useful buttons or information on the UI or screens unlike SH4, but we'll get back to that.

This game focuses on Roleplaying.. Which is great.. except for the fact, they went waaaay overboard in all the wrong ways... 

First Impression....

The tutoria.. It shows you how to move on the map.. Great.. It shows you how to shoot a non moving target... ehh?.. you quickly find upon continuation of playing the game, that infact, all the targets are NOT sitting still unlike the tutorial.. how do you aim? how do you compensate for the speed? WHO KNOWS! Tutorial certainly doesn't tell you... How do you turn on the automatic aiming like SH4? well even that is convoluted, and only about 40% accurate, even on targets that aren't trying to dodge and sailing straight... 

So now I got this completely lone merchant, which my crew seems unable to hit, so I manually put in the information myself and stop wasting torpedoes... (luckily for me I know how to do it from SH4, because the tutorial again... doesn't even tell you how to fucking shoot your only real weapon derp...) Hit it once, surface.. guns wont shoot... quick google search.. ah they're supposed to shoot automatically.. naturally, but they aren't... so lets do it myself.. so I run all the way to deck gun...

Ok, merchant down.. oh but now I got contact of a destroyer closing in, better dive and get away right? click dive 5-6 times.. nothing... ok? quick search on google.. Oh! of course.. simply telling my crew to dive isn't enough, I have to manually move my captain down too.. silly me.. of course he wouldn't just go down himself like in SH4... 

Alright, that's out of the way, turn my engines low to conserve battery power... but.. where's my fuel? hmm no clue.. it's not on the UI like in SH4 readily available.. naw lets try to run through the submarine like a captain on crack trying to get in everyones face to see who can tell me the fule levels.. oh wait but that information is on the most disgustingly horrible UI I've ever seen in a game.. let me just click FOUR TIMES to get to this information.. information I'll probably need to go too many times throughout my voyage to check fuel levels... 

You know.. it's not like the captain couldn't yell for fuel level checks.. but of course that'd require #logic... 

resurface the ship, only to find once again another vitally important thing is missing.. the battery levels... and they aren't recharging... Yet ANOTHER google search, only to learn 2 things.. 1) THey are supposed to be recharging automatically (Hint.. they aren't... yet another game breaking bug one of many I decided to leave out here cuz fuck this game anyway), and 2) the battery recharges faster the slower you're going and the less engines you're using.... Geee wouldn't that of been nice to have in the tutorial? 

Wanna rig for silent running? nope not a button the UI, or anything, you gotta run around in the sub finding the right person to toggle in the crew... 

You wanna pre heat your torpedoes? Load them? Charge the batteries? DO anything of importance? naw you can't just order it from the command room like any other sane naval commander would of done, you gotta run from 1 end of the submarine to the other non stop issueing non stop orders.... Why the fuck do I even have a crew? I'm doing everything myself anyway... 

This game.. is complete garbage.... 

1) UI elements that are critically important are missing from the screens they should be one like SH4. 

2) No quick ways to issue orders easily... 

3) AI is brain dead stupid

4) littered with game breaking bugs. (5 in 5 seperate attempts at a playthrough, all within the first hour) 

5) nonsensical UI

You should be paid by Ubisoft to play this game, not the other way around.

I should've read what others said before buying this.  It was so cheap and I wanted to play a submarine game, but life's too short to play rubbish like this.

Do not buy this game! I tried the game back in 2016 and it was a unplayable mess. I re-installed the game this afternoon to try out the Wolves of Steel mod, but I quickly found out a gold plated turd is still a turd. The TDC is useless, and the entire crew are apparently morons, because they can not identify enemy ships, and you can forget about speed and heading calculations. Of the roughly two dozen fish I launched, only two hit something. The first was a snap shot on a destroyer at point blank range, and the other was an acoustic that ignored the enemy ship and circled back to hit my sub, even though I was at zero throttle. Exit to Windows, control panel, UNINSTALL! This game is an infuriating abomination, and the Metacritic score of 3+/- is absolutely charitable. The best of the series remains Silent Hunter 3, with Silent Hunter 4 being a buggy but playable second. Stick with this two because this one is one of the worst games in a decade.

Bought it on sale, but wish I didn't.  At least I lost less .  I look forward to a sub sim with great graphics that plays like SH III.

Support is no longer available for this game, and Ubisoft, being Ubisoft, have no idea how to run a game or support, I am permanently logged out of my UPlay account because of a technicality and received no assistance. DO NOT BUY. I wish this was still a good game like it used to be, but I can no longer vouch for it.

Ahoi, sailor. Don't you wish you'd have a game so unfinished and broken, that you'll need to track no longer supported -- or even hosted -- mods just to have the crewmen get in the boat when you submerge? (No, seriously, I'm at 300 ft deep and I see the numbskulls on the conning tower with their binocculars through the periscope.) Don't you wish that the game would think you have a Tardis and your kills happened in month 0 of year 0, or, literal example I've seen, in month 39 of year 16183, so they don't count for a WW2 mission?

More importantly: Don't you wish you could live an accurate simulation of sailing on a big open ocean, with NOTHING HAPPENING FOR WEEKS AT A TIME? Don't you wish your time compression would be disabled every time a crew member as much as sees a fly, so it could all translate into hours of pointlessly wasting your life, just being bored out of your skull? Don't you wish you'd then have to redo the same boring patrol over and over again, because either the game crashed, or the required tonnage bar stopped updating, or some AI freighter crashed into you in port when you were coming back to hand in the quest? 

Because literally that'll be your experience when playing this game.

And for whoever is going for "but it's realistic!", so is spending half a year in training and another in a trench where nothing happens for infantrymen, but we don't make games about THOSE boring parts, do we? We skip to when shooty guy got to shoot someone, not make you live through every boring routine patrol where nothing happened.

Look, big world open games are only fun if you're going to do the Witcher 3 thing, where there might be a quest or some ruin to explore or such every hundred feet or less. Behind every rock, every bush, every bend in the road, or rickety hut door, there might be some interesting item, some quest giver, or the entrance to a secret dungeon, or just a few coins worth of loot. That's the only reason for me to run along some path in the woods like an idiot. If there is nothing for me to discover, then it's just boring, and you should let me just fast travel to my assigned patrol zone and be done with it.

And no, the time compression that keeps resetting if as much as the sonar guy thought he heard a fart is not it.

First mission with Wolves of Steel mod, get hit by destroyers with depth charges manage to get away, spend hours under water while the crew fixes damage. Sneak into Danzig at dark, sink a freighter, as I'm trying to leave get too close to it, it lands on me and sinks me. 

Going back to the crashes. I added mods and then went back to a saved game, crashed computer, it restarted. So if you do it wrong it will make you pay. You just have to be careful and yeah it may still crash now and again.

so IMERSIVE
this is the only game I know of that actually makes me feel like i'm actually in world war ll. and some basic characterization of the crew with a tone of camaraderie. and to that, the graphics are spectacular, the explosions are big and bright with debris flying across the sky. 


why this game makes all the PREVIOUS GAMES seem unplayably obsolete
being in first person AND being able to walk around your boat only adds adds further to the immersion but going back to being limited to set positions on the boat to teleport to and from becomes awkward. 

controls
the controls are so much better than previous versions. there are many improvements but most importantly. wasd > arrows, nuff said.

speaking of controls, one gripe I do have is that you really should have a FULL KEYBOARD.
If your keyboard doesn't have: delete, insert, page up and down. you cannot fully lower your periscopes (regular & tactical) making you stick out like a sore thumb while on the surface, will also force you to have to manually lower them below the water and is just annoying to know you can't do anything about it. A full numpad is also highly recommended, the numpad +, -, ., and 0 are needed for manual ship navigation and time compresion shortcuts.   

BUGS
It's not that bad. 
at first the game didn't even function, so googled a simple fix for it.go to the local game files, select the properties of the sh5 file, compatibility->change high DPI->override high DPI behavior.
There are many fixes for every issue and there is a lot of info online for every issue. Other than that launch problem, bugs are rare. Aside from the unsurprising glitches like running the game at 8192x speed is janky and the physics can occasionally be weird just like any other game from the era (It's amusing if you ask me). People have talked about the tonnage sunk counter not counting anymore, but I've not had those problems.
The game works better than people complain about it.

GAMEPLAY
assuming you like submarine games, this the peak, the top, the apex. This game has everything you could ever want in a sub sim. Even though the cycle of: search for target, prepare an ambush, torpedo, deck gun, and watch da big ka-boom, then repeat that process until you are out of torpedoes, return to dock for more torpedoes, repeat. Technically this repetitive cycle should get boring eventually, but I just can't stop having fun with it.

I played SH1 and SH2 back in the late 90's, early 00's. When I started playing SH5, I was taken aback by the brilliant sea detail! What also hit my memory from playing the earlier versions was the land that you thought should be there, was there in as equally great detail. I am truly impressed with what this studio has done with a game that is naturally immersive, but made it so realistic as well!
Works fine for me.

Seeing the pedigree set by the previous entries in the series, the lush graphics, and the hype surrounding being able to wander your submarine, you may make the mistake of buying this dumbed-down, buggy mess. Please don't. The graphic engine makes pretty wrapping paper, but it can't mask the scent of the turd it surrounds. The complete absence of any sort of QA process becomes immediately apparent upon recieving a mission brief and being told to select a mission. There are none. After stuffing around with your saved games to fix the problem, you will be instructed to sink 100k tons of shipping off the western approaches. Sink as many ships as you like, that progress bar is never going to fill up. You will find you need to wander around in first- person to give orders to individual sailors to get things done. This seems like a fine idea, until you realise that it takes three attempts on average to use a ladder, and getting stuck is so common that the devs generously bound a default hotkey to unstick you. controls are obtuse, buttons and shortcuts aren't where you might expect them to be, your boat is crewed by potatoes who seem to cease doing anything useful as soon as they're not in arm's reach, and the attack map "system" is a disgrace. When tied up inside a U-Boat pen, your boat bucks and rolls like it's in the mid atlantic in a gale. You can stand on the wharf, under a 7m concrete roof, next to your boat in maintenance, and press hotkeys to remotely raise and lower the periscopes, while your watch on deck search tirelessly for enemy ships. inside the sub-pen. These are the kind of no brainers which should be fixed before the game hits the shelf, except it seems Ubi got greedy, and wanted a half baked AAA blockbuster in record time. Speaking of Ubi, the game forces you to use u-play, a hate-crime of a DRM system which causes more issues than it solves. The only upside of this game is the tribe of rivet counting autists who tirelessly build mods out of sheer love for the genre. SH3 and 4 were good games that could be modded into great games. SH5 is an unplayable wreck that can be modded into a playable one. the problem with mods is they will just give you a taste for the game SH5 could have been, which will fill you with a sadness which can only be cured by playing 600 hours of silent hunter 3 with the GWX mod.
TL;DR: Get SH3 and the GWX 3.0 mod, pretend SH5 never existed. It's for the best.
With the “Wolf of steel” mod, this game is absolute a lot of fun! I have not come across alot of problems! Worth the money.

Silent Hunter 5 - A game that should’ve been sunk the day it was released.

I only paid the price of a cup of coffee for this steaming pile of crap, thank FK for that.
The Wolves of Steel made it playable but only on the ‘terms’ of the german creators that think the game ‘must’ be made so hard that it’s borderline unplayable unless you consider yourself an elitist ‘over-the-top’ simulator enthusiast that must have every bell and whistle available. And the never ending German talking/shouting is just too much for me to take anymore, it’s nauseating!

The graphics don’t seem much better than SH4, nor the gameplay.
The textures on the highest level are washed out and the physics are a mess.
The ‘TWoS’ FIX/addon was a nightmare to download from some basement in the middle of Eastern FK’n nowhere, then a hell of a lot of reading to even get it to work, but I did of course, and it seems all for nothing as I hated this game and it’s elitist gameplay style. (Oh and no, I don’t give a TOSS what any fan boi’s have to say to that! If this game floats your boat - here’s your paddle, now FK off)

On a side note, and a good one,,, I do regard Silent Hunter 4 as a top shelf simulator that can be toned down, both with the ‘wailing’ German speech and difficulty levels, plus expansions and skins etc to last a good while out on the ocean dodging tommie destroyers.

Ignore this SH5 garbage, which btw, should’ve been removed from Steam a long time ago as no one likes it and almost everyone seeks a refund after realising the game is faulty and needs an external patch to even see it FFS!

The uplay side of things totally sucks, the un modded game is stable, big Sh4 user and was a little dissapointed they didnt incorporate some of these modes as standard for Sh5. If you wanna play Das Boot, buy a 58" screen pump up the volume and add some 1930's music an\d get really really drunk while sinking tommies...awesome night :)
